english: 1. v. byaṅ (byang). — 2. S.g.? byaṅ-pa-sríṅ (byang pa sring) Sch.: an insect.
pf. of °byáṅ-ba ('byang ba) q.v.; byaṅ-séms (byang sems) a pure, holy mind Mil., prob. = byaṅ-čub-séms (byang chub sems).

1. inscription, direction, label. — 2. the tablet on which an inscription is written, záṅs-kyi byáṅ-bu-la (zangs kyi byang bu la) (to write) on a copper plate or tablet Glr.; yig-byáṅ (yig byang), k'a-byáṅ (kha byang), resp. źal-byáṅ (zhal byang), = byáṅ-bu (byang bu) 1; sgo-byáṅ (sgo byang) inscription over a door, dur-byáṅ (dur byang) on a sepulchre; rtags-byáṅ (rtags byang) a mark on a thing Cs.; brjed-byáṅ (brjed byang) list of marked luggage; miṅ-byáṅ (ming byang), resp. mtsan-byáṅ (mtshan byang) list of names Pth.; śog-byáṅ (shog byang) cards Sch.; byaṅ-rdó (byang rdo) a stone monument.

I. 1. Cs. proportion, symmetry, beauty, dpe-byad (dpe byad) Dzl., id.; byád-čan (byad can) well-proportioned, fair, beautiful; byad-méd (byad med) the contrary Cs. — 2. face, countenance Lex.; byad spus kẹ́ṅs-pa (byad spus kengs pa) a hairy face Glr.; byád-kyi bkrag (byad kyi bkrag) Thgy., mdaṅs (mdangs) Lt., brightness, radiancy, beautiful complexion; byad-bźín (byad bzhin) face Dzl., मुख Lex.; byad-gzúgs (byad gzugs) Sch.: stature, prob. more correctly: countenance and body Dzl. and elsewh. — II. (Cs. also byád-ma (byad ma)) 1. enemy. — 2. a wicked demon, byád-ma rmé-śa-čan (byad ma rme sha can) Wdli. — 3. also byad-stems (byad stems), S.O. and elsewh., imprecation, malediction, combined with sorcery, the name of an enemy being written on a slip of paper and hid in the ground, under various conjurations; gźán-gyi byad (gzhan gyi byad), pá-rol-poi byad-stéms (pha rol poi byad stems) a malediction practised by another; byád-du (byad du) or stéms-su jùg-pa (stems su 'jug pa), prob. to curse a person with conjurations. III. in compounds, yo-byád (yo byad), ča-byád (cha byad) q.v. — IV. frq. for byed (byed).
1. Ld. frq. for byá-na (bya na), *)an čò-k'an ('an co khang), ján-ma* (jan ma) cook. — 2. v. the following.

Cs. married man; Sch.: a free man, one divorced from his wife; byan-mo (byan mo) Cs. wife, spouse; Sch.: 1. a divorced woman. — 2. a whore. Only this latter signification seems to be known among the common people, e.g. *ä-pẹ ján-mo* (a pe jan mo), as a vulgar abusive term; byan-tsud-pa (byan tshud pa) Sch. 'to allure, entice, seduce'; these significations are, however, not sufficient to explain: byán-moi byi-bor (byan moi byi bor) (or -por (-por)) raṅ byan tsud (rang byan tshud) Lex., and: séms-la raṅ byan tsud (sems la rang byan tshud) Mil.

1. to clean, cleanse, wash, wipe, naṅ tams-čád-la (nang thams cad la) to clean the whole house Domaṅ. — 2. to take up, to gather with both hands, e.g. barley C.; byab-zed (byab zed) Sch. instrument for cleaning, brush; byabs-krus (byabs khrus) Sch. shower-bath.

1. kindness, love, affection, byáms-sems (byams sems) id. — 2. kind, loving, affectionate, used of the love of parents to their children, of the beneficent to the needy, but not in the contrary order, nor of love to inanimate objects; byáms-pai tiṅ-ṅe-°dzin (byams pai ting nge 'dzin) the meditation of love, compassion, frq.; mi kún-la byáms-śiṅ (mi kun la byams shing) being kind towards every body; byáms-pai ǰnyen (byams pai gnyen) kind, affectionate relations, frq.; byáms-pa maṅ-na (byams pa mang na) when I have many well-wishers, patrons Dom.; byáms-pa (byams pa) as a n. pr., also byáms-pa mgón-po (byams pa mgon po) Maitreya, the Buddha of the future period of the world, who at present is enthroned in the Galdan heaven, and who is frequently represented in pictures, v. Köpp.; byams-bźúgs (byams bzhugs) sitting like Maitreya, i.e. after European fashion on a chair, with his legs hanging down, opp. to tub-bźúgs (thub bzhugs), like Śākyathubpa; yet he is by no means uniformly represented in that posture.

supine of byéd-pa (byed pa); byar-méd (byar med) 1. prop.: non faciendum, not to be done. — 2. sbst. inactivity, inaction in the specifically Buddhist sense, apathy, indifference, byar-méd-kyi ṅáṅ-la gnas-par gyis (byar med kyi ngang la gnas par gyis) Thgr.

pf. of byéd-pa (byed pa); byás-na (byas na) 'si feceris', 'sin feceris', after a preceding prohibitive ma byed (ma byed) also to be rendered by else; as sbst. 1. 'factor'. 2. 'factus': byéd-pa byás-pa (byed pa byas pa) a doer of deeds, as the first grade of holiness; byás-pa śès-pa (byas pa shes pa), gzò-ba (gzo ba) Sch. to keep in mind a thing done, to requite, to reward; byas-čós (byas chos) Mil., also known in C., seems to be a notion akin to our conscience, *jhẹ-čọ̆ záṅ-po (jhe cho zang po), ṅẹ́m-pa* (ngem pa) C., *)jhẹ-lẹ́* (jhe le) id.
